steps.title: Find the total years worked by all 22 employees
steps.content:
Use the mean formula: sum of all values equals mean times the count.
$$\text{Sum}_{22} = 6.8 \times 22 = 149.6 \text{ years}$$

steps.title: Find the total years worked by the remaining 21 employees
steps.content:
After one employee leaves, the remaining 21 have a mean of 6.35 years:
$$\text{Sum}_{21} = 6.35 \times 21 = 133.35 \text{ years}$$

steps.title: Find the departing employee's years of service
steps.content:
Subtract the remaining sum from the original sum:
$$\text{Departing employee's years} = 149.6 - 133.35 = 16.25$$
D is correct.
